questions.q: In which city is the Qutb Minar located?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar stands in Delhi, within the Qutb complex in the Mehrauli area.

questions.q: Who began the construction of the Qutb Minar?
questions.explanation: Construction of the Qutb Minar was begun by Qutb-ud-din Aibak around 1199 CE.

questions.q: Which Sultan completed the Qutb Minar after Aibak?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish, the third Sultan of Delhi, completed the Qutb Minar started by Aibak.

questions.q: What type of structure is the Qutb Minar?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar is a soaring minaret, a tall tower of brick and stone.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar is associated with the early architecture of which medieval state?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar marks the early Indo-Islamic architecture of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar is famous for being the tallest such tower of its kind in which country?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar is the tallest minaret of its kind in India.

questions.q: Construction of the Qutb Minar began around which year?
questions.explanation: Aibak began the Qutb Minar around 1199 CE, in the earliest years of Sultanate rule in Delhi.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar today has the status of a:
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar and its surrounding complex are inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: After whom is the Qutb Minar named?
options: Qutb-ud-din Aibak (and/or the saint Qutbuddin Bakhtiyar Kaki)
questions.explanation: The tower's name is linked to Qutb-ud-din Aibak who began it, and to the Sufi saint Qutbuddin Bakhtiyar Kaki.

questions.q: The mosque built next to the Qutb Minar is called the:
questions.explanation: The Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque, among the earliest in India, adjoins the base of the Qutb Minar.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the Qutb Minar?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar rises to roughly 73 metres (about 240 feet), making it India's tallest minaret.

questions.q: How many storeys does the Qutb Minar have?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar has five distinct storeys, each marked by a projecting balcony.

questions.q: The lower storeys of the Qutb Minar are built mainly of which material?
questions.explanation: The earlier, lower storeys are of red sandstone, while upper portions add marble in later repairs.

questions.q: The surface of the Qutb Minar is decorated with bands of inscriptions in which script?
options: Arabic (Quranic verses) in Kufic and Naskh
questions.explanation: The tower carries decorative bands of Arabic calligraphy, including Quranic verses, in Kufic and Naskh scripts.

questions.q: Which famous corrosion-resistant artifact stands in the same Qutb complex as the Minar?
questions.explanation: The Gupta-era Iron Pillar, famed for not rusting, stands in the courtyard of the Qutb complex beside the Minar.

questions.q: Which later Sultan repaired the top storeys of the Qutb Minar after lightning damage in the 14th century?
questions.explanation: Firuz Shah Tughlaq restored the upper storeys after lightning strikes, adding marble in his repairs.

questions.q: An unfinished tower in the same complex, intended to be far larger than the Qutb Minar, was begun by which Sultan?
questions.explanation: Alauddin Khalji began the Alai Minar, meant to dwarf the Qutb Minar, but it was abandoned at its first storey.

questions.q: The ornate gateway built by Alauddin Khalji at the Qutb complex is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Alai Darwaza, built in 1311 by Alauddin Khalji, is a celebrated gateway in the Qutb complex.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar tapers as it rises, meaning its diameter:
questions.explanation: The minaret tapers, with its diameter shrinking from about 14.3 metres at the base to roughly 2.7 metres at the top.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar's fluted shaft alternates between which two profiles in its lower storeys?
questions.explanation: The lower storeys feature alternating angular and rounded flutings, giving the shaft its distinctive star-like section.

questions.q: Public access to climbing the interior staircase of the Qutb Minar was closed largely due to:
questions.explanation: After a stampede inside the tower in 1981 caused deaths, the interior was permanently closed to visitors.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar is generally described as a victory tower and a minaret attached to:
questions.explanation: The Minar functioned as a minaret of the adjacent Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque and as a tower symbolising victory.

questions.q: The Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque beside the Qutb Minar was constructed partly from materials of demolished temples of which faiths?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions and reused pillars show the mosque incorporated stonework from earlier Hindu and Jain temples on the site.

questions.q: An inscription on the Qutb Minar records that it was completed by which Sultan, who added storeys?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions credit Iltutmish with adding storeys and completing the tower begun by Aibak.

questions.q: The architectural style of the Qutb Minar is best described as:
questions.explanation: The Minar blends Ghurid/Afghan minaret forms with local Indian stone-carving traditions, an early Indo-Islamic synthesis.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar in the Qutb complex bears an inscription honouring a king named Chandra, dating to which era?
questions.explanation: The Iron Pillar's Sanskrit inscription honours a king Chandra and dates to the Gupta period, predating the Sultanate.

questions.q: The cupola that once topped the Qutb Minar, added by a British officer, was later removed and now sits in the grounds. It is popularly called:
questions.explanation: A cupola added by Major Robert Smith in the 1820s was removed in 1848 and now stands in the lawns as 'Smith's Folly'.

questions.q: The intended height of Alauddin Khalji's Alai Minar, had it been finished, was meant to be:
options: Exactly equal to the Qutb Minar
The same as the Iron Pillar
Roughly twice that of the Qutb Minar
questions.explanation: Alauddin planned the Alai Minar to be about double the Qutb Minar's height, but only its rubble core first storey was built.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar's purpose as a minaret was traditionally to:
options: Call the faithful to prayer (the adhan)
Serve as a defensive watchtower only
questions.explanation: As a minaret of the adjoining mosque, the tower was intended for the call to prayer, alongside its symbolic role.

questions.q: Which two upper storeys of the Qutb Minar are clad in marble, distinguishing them from the red sandstone below?
questions.explanation: Following Firuz Shah Tughlaq's repairs, the upper fourth and fifth storeys feature marble alongside sandstone.
